summary:
You have a network of n nodes labeled from 1 to n. You are given a list of directed travel times, where each entry times[i] = [u, v, w] means a signal sent from node u reaches node v after w units of time.

Starting by sending a signal from a given node k, return the time it takes for every node in the network to receive the signal. If one or more nodes can never be reached, return -1.

examples.explanation: From node 2, node 1 and node 3 are reached at time 1, and node 4 is reached at time 2. The slowest arrival is 2.
The signal travels from node 1 to node 2 in 1 unit of time, and all nodes are then reached.

constraints: times[i] = [u, v, w] with u != v and 1 <= u, v <= n
All directed edges (u, v) are unique
